meaning: n.碗碟洗涤处,碗碟储藏室
examples: If it wasn't, it was little Betty Butterworth, the scullery-maid.
"Martha," Mary said, "has the scullery-maid had the toothache again today?"
The relegation of France from top table to scullery would be an historic post-war shift.
In the scullery she found several fat demijohns of brown liquid bubbling through airlocks.
She later learnt that the matter that was fermenting in the scullery was nothing more than tea wine.
